notes: Hypothesis: a conservative baseline point with minimum allowed dense parallelism and lower precision can improve weighted score by materially reducing LUT/DSP/FF pressure and improving timing slack relative to dense2_dw16_fb7_base, while avoiding the current uncertainty around the pipelined conv variant. Expected latency impact: slower dense stage than dense2/dense5 candidates, so inference latency should increase versus higher DENSE_OUT_PAR points. Expected LUT/DSP/BRAM impact: LUT, FF, and DSP use should decrease from the 16-bit baseline; BRAM is expected to stay similar or unchanged because tensor sizes are not changing. Expected timing impact: best chance among current legal points to reduce critical-path pressure and move WNS toward non-negative slack at the same 100MHz target.
